data.title: Ethereum Price Declines Again, Consolidates Below $3K After Latest Breakdown
data.description:
Ethereum price failed to stay above $3,000 and tested $2,770. ETH is now attempting to recover but faces resistance near $2,880. Ethereum started a fresh decline after it failed to stay above $3,000. The price is trading below $3,000 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/eth/ethereum-price-declines-3k/

data.title: Strategy Stock Plunges Over MSCI Index Fears — Could $11.6B Exit Next?
data.description:
JPMorgan warns Strategy could face up to $11. 6 billion in outflows if MSCI and other index providers remove Bitcoin-heavy treasury firms from their equity indices.

The post Strategy Stock Plunges Over MSCI Index Fears — Could $11. 6B Exit Next? first appeared on Ecoinimist.

data.title: Jefferies Says Tether Is Quietly Driving Gold’s Record-Breaking Rally
data.description:
Jefferies says Tether’s rapid accumulation of more than 100 tons of gold has tightened supply and become a major driver of this year’s record-breaking gold rally.

data.title: The US Solana spot ETF saw a net inflow of $23.66 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the Solana spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $23.66 million yesterday (November 20th, Eastern Time). The SOL spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Bitwise SOL ETF (BSOL), with a net inflow of $20.12 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $444 million. This was followed by the Fidelity SOL ETF (FSOL), with a net inflow of $2.34 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $9.84 million. As of press time, the Solana spot ETF has a total net asset value of $745 million, a net asset value ratio of 1.00%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $500 million.

data.title: This week's top mining news highlights: Bitcoin mining company Cipher Mining plans to raise $333 million through a senior secured note issuance.
data.description: Week 47 of 2025 (November 14 - November 20): 1. Cloverpool did not update its 47-week hashrate data. 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $94,332, with a high of $99,836 and a low of $85,981, a decrease of 8.57% compared to the previous week's average price ($103,178). 2. Notable mining news includes: (1) Bitcoin mining company Cipher Mining plans to raise $333 million through the issuance of senior secured notes; (2) Bitcoin mining company MARA deposited 644 BTC worth $58.7 million into FalconX and Coinbase Prime; (3) Bitcoin mining company Bitfury announced a $1 billion investment plan focusing on ethical technology and AI innovation; (4) Bitcoin mining company CleanSpark spent $460 million to repurchase 30.6 million common shares; (5) American Bitcoin, a Bitcoin mining company related to the Trump family, released its Q3 financial report: it increased its holdings by approximately 3,000 BTC during the quarter.

data.title: Allegations of Market Manipulation in Bitcoin Derivatives
data.description: According to PANews, Mike Alfred, founder of Alpine Fox LP, has claimed that institutions are aggressively manipulating perpetual contracts and futures derivatives to suppress Bitcoin prices and intimidate investors into selling. Tom Lee, Chairman of BitMine, shared and agreed with Alfred's statement.

data.title: Citibank and Swift Complete Pilot for PvP Settlement Between Fiat and Digital Currencies
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Citibank and Swift have successfully completed a pilot project for Payment-versus-Payment (PvP) settlement between fiat and digital currencies. This initiative demonstrated the feasibility of interoperability between traditional financial systems and distributed ledger networks. The pilot was executed using the existing Swift infrastructure, seamlessly integrating institutional-grade blockchain connectors, business process coordinators, and smart contracts. Citibank utilized the Ethereum Sepolia testnet's test version of USDC to simulate a near-production environment.

data.title: Onchain Lens Reports Significant Losses in ETH Positions
data.description: According to Foresight News, monitoring by Onchain Lens reveals that the ETH long positions held by Machi Big Brother have faced partial liquidation once again, resulting in a loss of approximately $20.18 million.

data.title: SEC to Host Public Roundtable on Financial Monitoring and Privacy
data.description: According to PANews, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has announced a public roundtable discussion on financial monitoring and privacy issues. Scheduled for December 15, 2025, from 13:00 to 17:00 Eastern Time, the event will take place at the SEC headquarters in Washington, D.C. The roundtable aims to delve into policy matters related to these topics. The SEC encourages public participation through in-person registration and will also offer a live online broadcast of the event.

data.title: Irys announces IRYS token economics: 20% initial circulating supply, 8% allocated for airdrops and future incentives.
data.description: Irys, a programmable data chain, announced its IRYS token economics: The total supply of IRYS tokens is 10 billion, with an initial circulating supply of 20%. The allocation is as follows: Ecosystem 30%, Foundation 9.9%, Airdrop and Incentives 8%, Liquidity and Launch Partners 8%, Team and Advisors 18.8%, and Investors 25.3%. Team and investor tokens will be locked for the first year. A 2% inflation reward will be distributed to validators annually, halving every four years; 50% of execution fees and 95% of regular storage fees will be burned.

data.title: BitMart launches the "Contract Supernova" event, with a prize of 1 BTC.
data.description: According to official news, BitMart has launched the "Contract Supernova" event. During the event, users can earn chances to win prizes by completing tasks such as registration, advanced verification, net contract deposits, and trading volume. The prize pool includes rewards such as 1 BTC, 1 ETH, SOL, DOGE, and various tiers of USDT. The event has a guaranteed minimum reward to ensure that all participants receive a prize. Participants earn a chance to win a prize by completing their first contract trade with 1 USDT. Additionally, the event offers tiered tasks ranging from 100 USDT to 200,000 USDT, allowing users to participate according to their trading needs.

data.title: Opinion: Bitcoin is likely to find support at $81,800 and $74,800; a drop to $74,800 may mark the bottom of this bear market.
data.description: Trader Banmu Xia posted that Bitcoin currently has two high-probability bottoming levels: 81,800 and 74,800 (Coinbase price), as well as the range between these two prices. If it reaches 74,800, it could very well be the lowest point of this bear market.

data.title: A whale sold off some of its WBTC and liquidated its ETH holdings, incurring a loss of $26.348 million over four months.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684xtpa), a whale "cleared out its ETH holdings and sold some WBTC." In the past 8 hours, it sold 250 WBTC ($21.17 million) at an average price of $84,710, incurring a loss of $7.948 million. Its ETH holdings were liquidated in batches between November 17th and November 21st, ultimately selling 17,497 ETH ($52.41 million) at an average price of $2,994.9. The previously established 13,316 ETH position suffered a loss of $18.4 million. The whale's WBTC and ETH accumulated at the peak in August and September of this year have lost $26.348 million in less than four months, with the remaining 1,310 WBTC still showing a floating loss of $40.71 million.

data.title: Opinion: The recent decline in the crypto market was mainly driven by retail investors selling off Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs.
data.description: JPMorgan analysts say the recent pullback in the crypto market—particularly exacerbated by Bitcoin falling below the bank's estimated $94,000 production cost/support level—was primarily driven by retail selling of Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs, rather than crypto-native traders. "While crypto-native investors contributed to the market pullback in October through significant deleveraging (particularly in perpetual contracts), this deleveraging in perpetual contracts appears to have stabilized in November," wrote Nikolaos Panigirtzoglou, Managing Director at JPMorgan, and his team in a report on Wednesday. "Instead, the main force driving the continued pullback in the crypto market in November has been non-crypto investors, particularly retail investors who entered the crypto market through Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs." Analysts noted that retail investors have withdrawn approximately $4 billion from Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs so far this month, exceeding the record net outflows of February. This behavior contrasts sharply with retail investor flows in the stock market. Retail investors poured approximately $96 billion into equity ETFs (including leveraged products) in November—if this pace continues until the end of the month, the total will approach $160 billion, comparable to levels seen in September and October. They point out that retail investors have previously exhibited similar “divergent behavior”: aggressively buying stocks while simultaneously selling crypto ETFs in a few months (only February, March, and now November this year). This suggests that retail investors still view crypto assets and stocks as two separate asset baskets, even though both are risky assets.
